headline: PNP chief Marbil: Di kailangan magdeklara ng drug war | ABS-CBN News
text:
Sa unang pagharap sa media bilang bagong hepe ng Philippine National Police (PNP), inilatag ni Gen. Rommel Francisco Marbil ang kanyang mga nais isulong na patakaran sa organisasyon. 
Sa ilalaim ng kanyang termino, iginiit ni Marbil na hindi niya kailangang magdeklara ng war on drugs para magkaroon ng drug-free na komunidad.
Ayon sa opisyal, hindi niya ipag-uutos na magparamihan ng huli at kumpiska ng ilegal na droga.
"Ang gusto namin is ano talaga, hindi yung paramihan kasi napipilitan ka eh. Sige magparamihan tayo ng huli, pero you don’t solve the problem.. Now it’s more of key performance indicator based doon sa kanya-kanyang regions. Ano ba specific na problema ng regions mo then we will base yung sa performance mo," sabi ni Marbil.
"Wala po kaming giyera dito. Hindi ito giyera na kailangan. It’s more of talaga na papaano natin mapapababa yung crimes natin based doon sa mga parameters natin. I don’t want to say na may drug war, parang giyera na naman kami," dagdag niya.
7 dead after Mayor Baste declares 'war on drugs' in Davao City
VP Duterte 'no comment' on brother's deadly Davao City drug war

Nagbabala rin si Marbil sa mga pulis sa paggamit ng cellphone habang naka-duty.
"This is my first and last warning, no cellphone during duties. We need patrol, pagkanahuli ka naming nagse-cellphone, there will be no forgiveness. Very strict kami diyan. Gusto namin duty, duty, kapag patrol, patrol. Andito yung mga commanders natin. I need beat patrols, gusto ko maramdaman ng tao yung mga pulis natin sa baba," ani Marbil.
Hindi rin muna magpapatupad ng malawakang balasahan sa senior officers ang bagong hepe ng PNP.
"I do not do that kasi pagka ginawa mo yung reshuffle na 'yun magiging unstable yung unit eh... We will be coming up with a better parameter to be measured. Kung hindi mo kaya ito, then we will give you a chance to improve. Kung hindi mo talaga kaya, then we will get a better commander," paliwanag ni Marbil.
Sa halip na magpatupad ng internal cleansing, mas nais ng opisyal na magtakda ng pamantayan na magsisilbing gabay sa mga pulis.
"Yung internal cleansing always look at pulis natin na masama eh. Every time na nag-internal cleansing kami, laging sinasabi niyo changes. You know mababait yung mga pulis natin kaya nga sabi natin we go for leadership by example," sabi ni Marbil.
"Ang kailangan lang natin hindi po internal cleansing. We just come up with better measurements for our policemen na dapat ito ang standard na hahabulin niyo. You cannot cope with the standard then we will teach you how," dagdag niya.
Balak ni Marbil na ituloy ang mga magandang programang nasimulan na ng mga nakaraang PNP chief para mapababa ang bilang ng krimen sa bansa.
"We will just continue yung lahat ng nagawa ng mga predecessors natin... hindi kagaya dati na everytime there is a new chief PNP, he comes with new programs na narerealign yung mga anong priorities natin," sabi ni Marbil.
Tututukan din ng opisyal ang Anti-Cybercrime Group habang plano rin nitong mag-invest sa mga pasilidad at kumuha ng mga eksperto na makatutulong sa mga pulis na paigtingin ang kanilang mga hakbang para mapigilan ang cybercrime.
Marcos orders PNP: Address rising cybercrime cases
PNP nababahala sa pagtaas ng cybercrime cases
Sa unang Command Conference bilang PNP Chief sa Camp Crame kaninang umaga, binigyang diin ni Marbil sa mga pulis na gawin lang ang kanilang trabaho.
Nais din ng bagong upong hepe ng PNP na mas paigtingin ang kanilang serbisyo publiko para mas maramdaman aniya ng publiko na ligtas sila.

headline: Public urged: Avoid bidet use, frequent toilet flushing to save water | ABS-CBN News
text:
Jan Antonin Kolar/Unsplash 
MANILA — The government on Tuesday urged Filipinos to avoid frequently flushing toilets and using bidets to conserve water as the Philippines continued to grapple with dwindling water supply worsened by the El Niño.
There is no outright ban, but the government is encouraging the public to conserve water as much as they can, said Asec. Joey Villarama, spokesperson of the country’s El Niño Task Force.
“Kung minsan iihi tayo, hindi naman ganoon kasangsang yung amoy, puwede pa namang may gumamit pa bago mag-flush… Huwag po na every time gumamit ng palikuran ay mag-flush po agad,” he told reporters in Malacañang.
(Sometimes when we urinate, if the odor is not that foul, then another person can still use the toilet before flushing… We should not use the flush every time we use the toilet.)
“Kung kailangan na i-flush ‘yung inodoro ay mas mabuti po na gumamit na lamang ng balde,” he said. “Babaan po natin yung floater sa loob ng tangke para yung water level hindi masyado maaksaya o malaki yung gastos ng tubig.”  
(If there is a need to flush, it is better to use a pail. We can also lower the floater inside the water tank so that the water level needed for flushing will be kept at a minimum.)
More areas suspend in-person classes due to hot weather
Villarama discouraged the public from using bidets and showers.
“Gumamit po tayo ng tabo sa ating pagligo. Natuklasan ko noong panahon na nag-aaral pa ako na kasya po ang isang baldeng tubig sa pagligo,” the official said.
(Let use a dipper when taking a bath. I discovered when I was still a student that one pail of water is enough for one bath.)
“‘Yung mga bidet magastos yun kasi walang habas na pag-spray… walang pinagkaiba po yun sa paggamit ng garden hose,” he said.
(Bidets also use excessive amounts of water because of the liberal amount of water used when spraying… it’s no different from using a garden hose.)
“Ang pinakamainam po talaga ay gumamit ng tabo tuwing gagamit tayo ng palikuran para sukat at matipid ang pag gamit natin ng tubig,” he added.
(The best method is to use a water dipper whenever we use the comfort room because you can easily measure the amount of water being used.)
Cebu City mayor declares water crisis
The public should also be more “conscious” and “deliberate” when washing clothes, the spokesperson said.
“Mas matipid pa yung paggamit ng laundromat kaysa yung naglalaba tayo nang walang habas at nagbabanlaw tayo sa sarili nating bahay,” he said.
(More water is conserved when using laundromats instead of washing clothes manually at home.)
“Kapag po naglalaba in a public place, sa laundromat o sa bahay natin, gawin po natin yung conscious, deliberate effort na magtipid po sa tubig,” he said.
(When doing the laundry whether in a public place, at a laundromat or at home, we should also have a conscious, deliberate effort to conserve water.)
The government has also asked golf courses, subdivisions and condominiums to avoid cleaning their swimming pools and other facilities, he said.
They have also been asked to shut off their main water valves during off peak hours, he said.
Villarama said so far, the water level of the Angat Dam — which supplies water for Metro Manila and nearby areas — is at 198 meters or about 18 meters above the critical level.
Angat Dam water may reach critical level, PAGASA hydrologist says
El Niño damage hits P1.31 billion in mid-March: DA

headline: Quiboloy arrest: Davao police say Senate security sought help from Camp Crame | ABS-CBN News
text:
Pastor Apollo Quiboloy. Photo by Fernando Sepe Jr., ABS-CBN News
(UPDATED) The Davao police said the Senate security office has requested for police assistance from the Philippine National Police (PNP) headquarters in Camp Crame in relation to the implementation of an arrest order against Apollo Quiboloy.
Davao police spokesperson Police Captain Hazel Tuazon said that the local police have not received a copy of the arrest order against Quiboloy. 
The Senate has issued an arrest order for the televangelist for failing to attend hearings on his alleged human trafficking and sexual abuse crimes.
Quiboloy can't refuse Senate summons: Carpio
"The Police Regional Office 11 is directed to assist the OSSA in implementing the contempt warrant," Tuazon told the local media.
Davao City police on alert for Quiboloy's arrest
The Davao police said they have no idea yet on when the OSSA will arrive in Davao City to implement the arrest order.
Local authorities also said they are still clueless on Quiboloy's whereabouts.
Tuazon said they are not trying to hide or protect Quiboloy, as it has been 2 weeks since the arrest order was signed by Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri.
Davao police clueless on Quiboloy's whereabouts

headline: Internet voting set for overseas voters in 2025 polls | ABS-CBN News
text:
Internet voting will be the primary mode of voting for overseas Filipinos during the 2025 midterm elections. 
This is the plan the Commission on Elections (Comelec) laid out as it held the second round of bidding for the P465.8-million Online Voting and Counting System (OVCS), the second largest contract for next year’s polls.
“Magaganap lamang ang ating sinasabi na in-person voting or mail-in voting doon sa mga bansa na hindi pinapayagan ang pag-conduct ng internet voting gaya nga po sa China, sa Russia at sa Israel,” Comelec spokesperson Atty. John Rex Laudiangco said at the sidelines of the event.
(In-person voting or mail-in voting will only happen in countries where internet voting is restricted such as China, Russia, and Israel). 
Out of 92 posts abroad, there are 17 countries with internet restrictions and Comelec is still trying to talk to their governments to find out if the rules are flexible.
The poll body will also determine the mode of casting votes, whether internet voting, via mail, or in-person, depending on the rules per country and the distance of a voter from the embassy or consulate.
According to Laudiangco, they opted to shift to internet voting for overseas Filipinos for convenience and hopefully, a better voter turnout.
“Ito po yung talagang hiling po ng ating mga kababayang OFWs. Una, yung ating mga seafarers at marino na hindi sa lahat ng pagkakataon tuwing halalan, na nasa lupa sila para makaboto sa mga konsulada o kaya po sa embahada,” he said.
(This is what our OFWs requested. First, our seafarers during elections, are not always on land to cast their vote in consulates or embassies.)
The Comelec also hopes that the more than 600,000 voter turnout in 2022 will increase. There are 1.6 million registered overseas Filipino voters during the last presidential elections. 
Comelec targets 3 million registered overseas Filipinos for midterm polls
SAFETY OF INTERNET VOTING 
An overseas voter has to enroll one gadget to vote online. Laudiangco said the platform would be like a smartphone application that is easy to navigate.
“Sisiguraduhin po ng Comelec na napakadali po at user-friendly ang ating pagboto,” he said. “Kung tayo po ay nakakagamit ng smartphone, nakapagpi-Facebook, nakapag-cha-chat po tayo, at lalong lalo na kung nakakapag-online banking… ganoon kasimple lang din po ang gagawin ng Comelec sa internet voting.”
(Comelec will ensure that this will be easy and user-friendly. Internet voting will be as easy as using a smartphone, Facebook, chatting, and doing online banking.)
Laudiangco also guaranteed the safety and security of the system they would procure.
Aside from enrolling for internet voting to authenticate one’s personal information and gadget, a voter will also be required to have a PIN. Facial recognition is not a requirement but it is a feature that will be present in the system.
“Kung napagkakatiwalaan niyo ang mga financial at banking institutions sa inyong mga pera ay mapagkakatiwalaan niyo rin po ang sistemang pino-procure ng Comelec dahil ang priority po dito ay security at auditability,” he added. 
(If you trust financial and banking institutions with your money, you can also trust the system that Comelec is procuring because our priority is security and auditability.)
A voter receipt will be shown through the app after voting to ensure that the correct votes were recorded.
Should voters encounter difficulties, they could still go to embassies o consulates where there would be a kiosk for internet voting.
Overseas Filipino voters will be given 30 days before the May 12 elections to cast their votes.
Four companies submitted their bids for the OVCS, including Indra Soluciones Technologias De La Information, S.L.U.; the joint venture of SMS Global Technologies, Inc. and Sequent Tech Inc.; joint venture of Voatz Inc., EPLDT, Inc., and eBizolution, Inc.; and joint venture of AMA Group-Dasan-Kevoting.
Their eligibility will be determined as their bids were opened earlier on Tuesday. 
NSC sounds alarm on possible foreign interference in 2025 midterm polls
OTHER CONTRACTS 
Other contracts for the 2025 midterm elections include the Full Automation System with Transparency Audit/Count (FASTrAC) and transmission.
The joint venture of South Korean firm Miru Systems Company is currently in the customization phase for the FASTrAC project.
Miru bags P18.8-B project for May 2025 elections
South Korean company denies poor track record in elections
The Special Bids and Awards Committee, meanwhile, has submitted its recommendation to the Comelec En Banc to award the transmission contract to iOne Resources, Inc., after finding it compliant. The committee is awaiting the final decision on this.
5 House bills seek to weed out 'placeholder' candidates during election

headline: BI: 4 suspects behind fraudulently acquired PH IDs arrested in Palawan | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The Bureau of Immigration said 4 Chinese who were allegedly behind fraudulently acquired government IDs were arrested in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan last month.
In a statement, BI intelligence division chief Fortunato Manahan Jr. identified the four suspects as Wang Tao, Li Xiaoming, Guo Zhi Yang and Lyu Zhiyang, who were arrested in Brgy. San Pedro.
The BI said the operation was conducted in coordination with the National Intelligence Coordinating Agency and the Armed Forces of the Philippines.
 READ: BI warns public vs fake eTravel websites, social media scams
Lyu, also known as Ken Garcia Lee, was allegedly the mastermind of the operation and earned notoriety in the local community after being tagged as the group’s leader in Palawan, said the BI.
Lyu supposedly heads the local syndicate providing illegally obtained government-issued documents to undesirable aliens and trafficking victims.
Several identification cards issued by the Philippine government such as drivers’ licenses, postal IDs, and birth certificates were found and seized from the suspects.
“The arresting team of the operation have been working on the case for several months and have kept close coordination with intelligence forces and law enforcement agents to ensure the smooth arrest of the subjects,” said Manahan.
The BI noted that last year, there were at least 10 incidents of arrests involving foreigners who presented authentic Philippine IDs.
Tansingco also renewed his call to local government units to report to the BI cases of foreign nationals conducting illegal activities in their areas such as identity fraud.
“These foreign nationals use Philippine documents to avoid inspection, buy property, or even interfere in politics,” said Tansingco.
The BI said the 4 suspects, who are facing charges for misrepresentation and undesirability in violation of the Philippine Immigration Act of 1940, were already transferred to the BI facility in Bicutan, Taguig City.
READ: 8 telecom fraud suspects deported from Philippines arrested in Japan

headline: Pamamaril sa taxi driver, huli sa CCTV | ABS-CBN News
text: Huli sa CCTV ang pamamaril sa isang taxi driver ng dalawang lalaki na sakay ng motorsiklo. Sa video sinusundan ng taxi ang motor nang bumaba ang angkas nito at pinaputukan ang taxi. Dead on the spot ang taxi driver. Hinihinalang onsehan sa ilegal na droga ang motibo sa krimen.

text: The Philippine defense chief denounces China's alleged attempts to deflect attention away from its illegal activities in the Philippines' exclusive economic zone. - The World Tonight, ANC, April 2, 2024

headline: Mga Pinoy huwag magpaloko sa propaganda ng China: Teodoro | ABS-CBN News
text: Hinimok ni Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. ang mga Pilipino na huwag magpaloko sa propaganda ng China, habang iginiit na dapat may managot sa umano'y "gentleman's agreement" kaugnay ng BRP Sierra Madre na nakasadsad sa Ayungin Shoal. Iminungkahi naman ni dating Presidential spokesperson Harry Roque na tuluyan nang iretiro sa serbisyo ng militar ang BRP Sierra Madre para humupa ang tensyon sa West Philippine Sea. Pero tutol dito si dating Senador Antonio Trillanes IV. Nagpa-Patrol, Bianca Dava. TV Patrol, Martes, 2 Abril 2024

headline: Mga nagsuspende ng face-to-face classes dahil sa init, nadagdagan | ABS-CBN News

Hinimok naman ng Department of Labor and Employment ang mga employer at negosyante na mag-abot ng tulong sa mga manggagawa na apektado ng labis na init. Nagpa-Patrol, Joyce Balancio. TV Patrol, Martes, 2 Abril 2024

headline: Parents-teachers group ready to donate electric fans to public schools | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The National Parents-Teachers Association Philippines (NPTA) on Tuesday expressed its willingness to help public schools by donating additional electric fans to help alleviate heat inside the classrooms.

"With regard to electric fans, ang nagpo-provide talaga ay PTA [Parents-Teachers Association]. Ang PTA officers ang pino-project agad ay electric fans, ceiling fans, stand fan and wall fan," NPTA Executive Vice President Lito Senieto told ABS-CBN News.   
"Iyong mga PTA officer, upon the recommendation of the chair, pwede naman po uli magkaroon ng project na naman," he added. 
Senieto said some parents have already volunteered to donate individually to ensure the comfort of their children. 
"May mga parents din na iyong kanilang mga anak, nasa dulo, hindi na tinatamaan ng hangin ng electric fan. Iyon din po nagboboluntaryo rin na mag-donate ng electric fan. Ang PTA nagaayos din ng mga sirang electric fan," he said.

The Department of Education (DepEd) earlier assured schools of funds that could be used to purchase additional equipment or appliances that can fight heat inside the classrooms. 
"Mayroon naman po tayong binababang mga MOOE [maintenance and other operating expenses] sa ating mga schools para siguraduhin na kung nasira man ang electric fan ay mapagawa nila. Ventilation sa ating schools ay something very important," DepEd Undersecretary Michael Poa told ABS-CBN News on Monday.

On Tuesday, more schools declared suspension of face to face classes and have shifted to alternative delivery modes such as modular learning, and online classes. 
These are the schools and local government units that made announcements as of 3:00 p.m. of April 2, 2024, according to DepEd. 
 
RELATED VIDEO:

headline: SAPUL SA CCTV: Taxi driver pinagbabaril sa Malabon | ABS-CBN News
text: Sapul sa CCTV ang pamamaril at pagpatay ng riding-in-tandem sa isang taxi driver sa Barangay Potrero, Malabon City. Onsehan sa ilegal na droga ang isa sa tinitingnang anggulo ng PNP sa krimen. Nagpa-Patrol Lyza Aquino. TV Patrol, Martes, 2 Abril 2024

headline: Tulfo, VP Duterte statistically tied as 2028 presidential frontrunners: Pulse Asia | ABS-CBN News
text:
Sen. Raffy Tulfo and Vice President Sara Duterte. Senate PRIB/File
MANILA — Sen. Raffy Tulfo and Vice President Sara Duterte are statistically tied in first place in the latest Pulse Asia Survey for the 2028 presidential elections.
READ: Marcos suffers double digit losses in approval, trust ratings: Pulse Asia
Pulse Asia asked 1,200 respondents nationwide from March 6 to 10 the question: "Who would you vote for if the 2028 national elections were held today?"
Tulfo at 35 percent had a 1 percent lead over Duterte's 34 percent. Pulse Asia President Ronald Holmes said this is a statistical tie, citing the survey's +/-3 margin of error.
Tulfo enjoyed huge margins over Duterte in the National Capital Region (41 to 26), Balance Luzon (37 to 23), and Visayas (46 to 20) and Classes ABC (36-22) and E (46 to 28) while Duterte led Tulfo in Mindanao (72 to 18) and Class D (37 to 33).
Former Vice President Leni Robredo was a distant 3rd at 11 percent, followed by Sen. Imee Marcos at 5 percent, former Sen. Manny Pacquiao at 3 percent, Sen. Robin Padilla at 2 percent, Sen. Risa Hontiveros at 1 percent, and Speaker Martin Romualdez at 0.5 percent.
Others mentioned were former President Rodrigo Duterte (0.2 percent) and former Manila Mayor Isko Morendo Domagoso (0.1 percent)
Seven percent of those polled couldn't give an answer or refused to give an answer.
Meanwhile, Sen. Grace Poe, who lost the 2016 presidential elections, topped the Vice Presidential poll at 35 percent, with Sen. Marcos a distant second at 16 percent. Pacquiao was at 14 percent, Padilla at 14 percent, Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri at 7 percent, Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro at 4 percent, and Romualdez at 1 percent.
SENATE BETS
The same Pulse Asia survey also showed that two Tulfo brothers are in the winning circle for the 2025 senatorial elections.
ACT-CIS Rep. Erwin Tulfo enjoys a 57.1 percent voter preference, with another Tulfo close behind.
Rep. Tulfo is followed by former Senate President Vicente Sotto III at 51.8 percent, former President Duterte at 47.7 percent, Sen. Bong Go at 44.2 percent, Sen. Pia Cayetano at 37.7 percent, Pacquiao at 33.7 percent, Sen, Ronald dela Rosa at 33.2 percent, Sen. Marcos at 32.1 percent, Domagoso at 31.5 percent, broadcaster Ben Tulfo at 30.5 percent, Sen. Ramon Revilla at 29.6 percent, Makati Mayor Abby Binay at 29.1 percent, former Sen. Panfilo Lacson at 28.6 percent, TV host Willie Revilliame at 25.9 percent, Sen. Lito Lapid at 25.4 percent, and Dr. Willie Ong at 24.6 percent.
RELATED VIDEO

headline: Selos, agawan ng pasahero sinisilip sa pagpatay sa pedicab driver | ABS-CBN News
text: Patay ang senior citizen na pedicab driver matapos pagsasaksakin ng nakaalitang tricycle driver sa Sampaloc, Maynila. Selos at agawan umano ng pasahero ang ilan sa tinitingnang anggulo ng krimen. Nagpa-Patrol, Karen de Guzman. TV Patrol, Martes, 2 Abril 2024

headline: DENR suspends ECC applications for projects within protected areas | ABS-CBN News
text:
MANILA — The Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) has ordered the suspension, until further notice, of all applications for an environmental compliance certificate (ECC) for projects within protected areas.
READ: ‘Yung Chocolate Hills iniingatan po namin’: Manager of controversial Bohol resort speaks up
In a memorandum from the Office of DENR Secretary Toni Yulo-Loyzaga addressed to all undersecretaries for field operations, bureau directors, regional executive directors and directors, a list of directives should be implemented to make sure there is “stricter oversight and adherence to environmental regulations within protected areas.”
The directives include the conduct of an inventory of existing structures within protected areas and an evaluation of the existing structure’s compliance to environmental regulations and standards. A comprehensive review of all ECCs issued for projects within protected areas should also be reviewed.
“All pending and future ECC applications for projects within PAs processed at the Regional Offices shall be submitted to the Environment Management Bureau Central Office for final review and approval,” the memorandum read.
The announcement of the suspension was made during Loyzaga’s visit to Mt. Apo Natural Park on Tuesday where she also discussed the next steps on Mt. Apo Natural Park and other protected areas.
READ: Viral Chocolate Hills resort ordered closed due to violation: DENR
In March, lawmakers questioned the operation of a resort amidst Bohol’s Chocolate Hills without an ECC. Captain’s Peak Resort, since going viral, has stopped operations.
RELATED VIDEO

headline: Semana Santa, taimtim na ginunita ng overseas Pinoys | ABS-CBN News
text:
PARIS -  Sabay-sabay na iwinagayway ng mga mananampalataya ang mga palaspas kasabay ng pag-awit ng Hosanna at pagbabasbas ng obispo sa labas ng Charismatic Episcopal Church Paris.
“Ang Holy Week is like a pilgrimage, we walk with Christ today, we entered Jerusalem with Him so we might join Him in His resurrection and I pray that it will bring a restoration and a transformation of our life,” sabi ni Bishop Elmer Belmonte,Charismatic Episcopal Church Europe. 

Aniya, sana raw ngayong Semana Santa ay maging daan para sa mga Pilipino sa Paris na manumbalik at lumakas pa ang kanilang pananampalataya sa Diyos. 
Idiinaan naman sa sayaw ng mga kabataan ang mensahe sa pagsagip ni Kristo sa mga kasalanan ng sanlibutan at pagpapalago sa buhay Kristiyano.
Sa Saint Bernadette's church sa Paris naman halos pitong daang debotong Pilipino ang nagbitbit ng kanilang palaspas na benindisyunan ni Father Leo Balaguer.
ABU DHABI, UAE 
Bago ang Semana Santa, hindi rin nakalimutang ipagdiwang ng mga Pilipino sa Gitnang Silangan ang kapistahan ng Poong Jesus Nazareno.

Sa Abu Dhabi, pinamunuan ni Fr. Troadio delos Santos ng Saint Joseph's Cathedral Abu Dhabi, kasama ang Senyor Mangbubuklod Balangay Minor Basilica of the Black Nazarene ang pagdiriwang. 

“Itong debosyon na’to ang siyang kinakapitan namin para mas lalo kaming mapalapit sa Panginoon at maiwas kami sa mga bagay na hindi maganda para sa aming buhay,” sabi ni Nestor Awitin Jr, organizer at miyembro ng Senyor Mangbubuklod Balangay, MBBN-UAE
May espesyal namang dalangin ang ilang mga deboto. 

“Ako po’y isang cancer survivor, ako po ay pinagaling ng Senyor Nazareno. Third Chemo pa lang, alam ko na, kasi nagpakita na sya sa akin sa panaginip,” sabi ni Che Dela Cruz, breast cancer survivor.
Pero higit sa hiling, hinikayat ng simbahan ang tunay na debosyon.
“Makikita tayo ni Hesus na naipapakita natin si Hesus sa ating mga kababayan sa pamamagitan ng ating malalim at tunay na pananampalatayang Katoliko dito sa Gitnang Silangan,” sabi ni Fr. Troadio Delos Santos, OFM Cap, assistant parish priest ng Saint Joseph’s Cathedral, Abu Dhabi. 


Nagkaroon naman ng “dress like Sto.Niño” sa Saint Mary’s Catholic Church sa Dubai.
Pinangunahan ang selebrasyon ng United Cebuanos-Abu Dhabi and Dubai chapter.
“Even wala kami sa Cebu, still we continue the legacy, and we are very proud of it because Sinulog is the grandest festival of all the festivals in the Philippines,” sabi ni Aldwin “Jlo” Guardiana, former United Cebuano in the UAE chairman. 

“Isa din yan sa ipinagdadasal ko sa Panginoon, specially to Señor Sto. Niño na pupunta ako ng Abu Dhabi, please guide me Señor Sto. Niño at dadalhin kita sa Abu Dhabi,” sabi ni Egie Pepito Esma “Kawaii”, United Cebuano core group chairman, Abu Dhabi chapter. 
May mensahe rin parish priest sa lahat ng mananampalataya.
“Hiling ko lang sa inyo, huwag niyong kontrahin ang plano ng Diyos. Dahil kapag sinunod mo ito. Ikaw ay magiging masaya at mas mabibiyayaan,” sabi ni Fr Chito Bartolo, parish priest ng St. Joseph Cathedral. 
“Ngayon saan may Pilipino, doon man sa pinakamalamig na lugar o pinakamainit na lugar, sigurado may dala-dalang Sto. Niño, Pit Señor,” sabi ni Fr Troy, assistant parish priest. 

Sa Gitnang Silangan man o Europa, hindi pa rin tinatalikuran ng mga debotong Pilipino ang tradisyon ng Simbahang Katolika para mas lalo pang mapalakas ang kanilang pananampalataya. 
(Kasama sina Cory de Jesus at Bong Agustinez sa France at Jose Barbasa at Raffy Reyes sa United Arab Emirates).
Para sa mga nagbabagang mga balita tungkol sa ating mga kababayan sa Europa, Gitnang Silangan at Africa, tumutok sa TFC News sa TV Patrol.
